title: Demand Planning Brief — Bond-Repair Leave-In Cream Launch

sections.heading: Product Overview
sections.content:
Product Name: Bond-Repair Leave-In Cream
Product Type: Leave-In Hair Treatment (Cream)
SKU Count: 2

SKU Details:
• SKU 1 — Travel Size: 75mL
• SKU 2 — Full Size: 150mL

Key Active Ingredient: Bis-aminopropyl dimaleate
Formula Stage: [To be confirmed]
Packaging Finalization Date: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Initial Stocking Quantities
sections.content:
Launch stocking units by SKU:
• Full Size (150mL): 5,000 units
• Travel Size (75mL): 2,000 units
• Total Launch Units: 7,000 units

Note: These figures represent initial stocking quantities for the salon distribution phase. Ulta stocking quantities to be determined ahead of the 6-month retail expansion.

sections.heading: Channel Strategy & Timeline
sections.content:
Phase 1 — Salon Distribution:
• Channel: Professional salon network
• Timeline: Launch phase (Month 1)
• Target Units: 7,000 units (5,000 full size + 2,000 travel)

Phase 2 — Ulta Beauty Retail Expansion:
• Channel: Ulta Beauty (mass prestige retail)
• Timeline: Approximately 6 months post salon launch
• Stocking quantities for Ulta: [To be confirmed]
• Planogram and shelf placement requirements: [To be confirmed]

Demand Planning Action: Forecast for Ulta onboarding must be initiated no later than Month 3 to ensure sufficient inventory buffer and manufacturing lead time alignment.

sections.heading: Demand Forecast Notes
sections.content:
• Initial demand signal is based on planned channel stocking, not sell-through velocity data (to be updated once POS data is available from salon partners)
• Sell-through rate assumptions: [To be confirmed]
• Reorder trigger points per SKU: [To be confirmed]
• 12-month volume forecast: [To be confirmed]

title: Supply Risk & Lead Time Assessment — Bond-Repair Leave-In Cream

sections.heading: Key Ingredient Supply Profile
sections.content:
Ingredient at Risk: Bis-aminopropyl dimaleate
Role in Formula: Primary bond-repair active
Current Supplier Geography: European Union (single-source)
Supplier Count: 1 (sole source — high risk)
Supplier Lead Time: 8–10 weeks from purchase order placement

Risk Classification: HIGH
• Single-source dependency creates a critical vulnerability in supply continuity
• EU-based sourcing introduces potential exposure to import delays, customs clearance variability, and currency fluctuation impacts on cost

sections.heading: Lead Time Impact on Planning Horizons
sections.content:
Given an 8–10 week ingredient lead time, the following planning windows apply:

• Minimum raw material order trigger: 10 weeks before required manufacturing start date
• Recommended safety buffer: 12–14 weeks ahead of manufacturing start to account for customs, QC release, and logistics variability
• Effective demand planning freeze horizon: Orders must be confirmed at least 12 weeks before any planned production run

Implication for Ulta Launch (Month 6):
• Raw material orders for Ulta stocking inventory must be placed no later than Month 3–4 to ensure on-time manufacturing
• Demand forecast for Ulta must be locked by end of Month 3

sections.heading: Q4 Holiday Demand Spike Risk
sections.content:
Anticipated Demand Event: Q4 Holiday Gift Set Spike
Impacted SKUs: Both Travel (75mL) and Full Size (150mL) — travel size especially relevant for gift set inclusion

Risk Assessment:
• Q4 demand surge combined with 8–10 week ingredient lead time creates a high-risk window if planning is not initiated in Q2
• If product launches in Q1–Q2, the first Q4 will be the highest-risk replenishment cycle
• Gift set configurations and bill of materials for holiday sets: [To be confirmed]
• Incremental Q4 volume uplift estimate: [To be confirmed]

Recommended Actions:
• Place Q4 safety stock ingredient order by end of Q2
• Align with contract manufacturer on Q4 production capacity reservation
• Confirm gift set packaging components lead times separately — these may introduce additional risk

sections.heading: Supply Risk Mitigation Recommendations
sections.content:
• Immediately qualify a secondary or backup supplier for bis-aminopropyl dimaleate to eliminate single-source dependency
• Negotiate consignment stock or vendor-managed inventory (VMI) arrangement with current EU supplier to reduce effective lead time
• Establish a minimum safety stock level for the active ingredient equivalent to at least one full production run
• Implement a rolling 13-week demand and supply review cadence
• Set up supply alerts if ingredient inventory falls below [To be confirmed] kg threshold
• Assess airfreight feasibility vs. sea freight cost trade-off for urgent replenishment scenarios

sections.heading: Open Items for Supply Chain Team
sections.content:
• Secondary supplier qualification timeline: [To be confirmed]
• Minimum order quantity (MOQ) for bis-aminopropyl dimaleate: [To be confirmed]
• Contract manufacturer location and production lead time: [To be confirmed]
• Finished goods warehousing location and 3PL partner: [To be confirmed]
• Inbound freight mode (air vs. sea) and standard transit time: [To be confirmed]

sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory aids team before taking action.

title: Seasonal & Promotional Demand Calendar — Bond-Repair Leave-In Cream

sections.heading: Annual Demand Event Overview
sections.content:
The following demand events have been identified as significant planning considerations for the Bond-Repair Leave-In Cream line:

• Q4 Holiday Season (October–December): Primary seasonal spike driven by holiday gift set inclusion. Both SKUs expected to see elevated demand. Travel size (75mL) is a strong candidate for gift set bundling.
• Ulta Beauty Launch (Month 6 — estimated): Retail launch will generate a one-time stocking demand event distinct from organic replenishment demand.
• Salon Launch (Month 1): Initial trade load-in across salon distribution network.

Additional promotional events to be confirmed:
• New Year / Hair Resolutions (January): [To be confirmed — potential promotional opportunity]
• Spring Refresh (March–April): [To be confirmed]
• Back-to-School / Fall Refresh (August–September): [To be confirmed]
• Ulta 21 Days of Beauty or promotional events: [To be confirmed — requires Ulta vendor calendar alignment]

sections.heading: Q4 Holiday Planning Detail
sections.content:
Event: Holiday Gift Set Season
Demand Classification: HIGH IMPACT — Confirmed seasonal spike
Impacted SKUs: Travel Size (75mL) primarily; Full Size (150mL) secondarily

Planning Timeline for Q4 Readiness:
• By End of Q1: Confirm gift set configurations, bundle SKUs, and BOM
• By End of Q2: Lock Q4 volume forecast; place raw material POs for bis-aminopropyl dimaleate with EU supplier (accounting for 8–10 week lead time + buffer)
• By Start of Q3 (July): Confirm production slot with contract manufacturer for holiday inventory
• By End of Q3 (September): Finished goods for Q4 holiday sets in warehouse; safety stock for replenishment confirmed
• October–December: Active selling season; monitor sell-through weekly

Estimated Incremental Q4 Volume: [To be confirmed]
Gift Set Retail Price Point: [To be confirmed]
Retailer(s) for Gift Set Distribution: Ulta Beauty + Salon Channel (if applicable)

sections.heading: Promotional Uplift Assumptions
sections.content:
• Baseline weekly velocity per SKU (salon): [To be confirmed]
• Baseline weekly velocity per SKU (Ulta): [To be confirmed]
• Q4 uplift multiplier vs. baseline: [To be confirmed — industry benchmark for prestige haircare gift sets: 1.5x–3x baseline]
• Ulta launch week uplift vs. steady-state: [To be confirmed — typical retail launch load-in: 2x–4x ongoing replenishment]

Note: All uplift assumptions must be validated against final retailer commitments and historical analogs from comparable bond-repair or treatment product launches.

sections.heading: Demand Planning Review Cadence
sections.content:
Recommended review schedule:
• Weekly: Short-term supply vs. demand alignment (rolling 6 weeks)
• Monthly: Full SKU-level demand review; reorder point validation; supplier lead time check
• Quarterly: Strategic forecast update; seasonal volume confirmation; Ulta performance review
• Pre-Q4 (August): Dedicated holiday demand planning review with full cross-functional team (marketing, supply chain, sales, finance)
